ACK_MODAL_MSG_CONNECTED: Connection to the interface has been restored.
ACK_MODAL_MSG_DISCONNECTED: Connection to the interface has been lost.
ACK_MODAL_MSG_NO_DATA: The system is not receiving data from the interface.
ACK_MODAL_MSG_NO_STATUS: The interface is not reporting its status.
AM_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this asset?
AM_SET_EDITOR_DROP_OVERLAY: Drop files here to add them to the set
AM_SET_EDITOR_EMPTY: No images in set. Add some images to get started.
AM_SET_EDITOR_ERR_REQUIRED: Please enter a set name and add at least one image.
AM_UPLOAD_HINT: Drag & Drop assets here for upload
AS_TTS_NOT_SUPPORTED: Text-to-speech not supported in this browser.
CD_CONFIRM_EXIT_MESSAGE: Are you sure you want to exit to home?
DBM_CONFIRM_DELETE_MSG: Are you sure you want to delete database '{{name}}'? This action cannot be undone.
DBM_CONFIRM_RESET_MSG: Are you sure you want to reset the current database to factory defaults? This will erase all data and cannot be undone.
DBM_CONFIRM_RESET_MSG_1: Are you DANGEROUSLY sure you want to RESET "{{name}}" to factory defaults? ALL DATA WILL BE LOST.
DBM_CONFIRM_SWITCH_MSG: Are you sure you want to switch to "{{name}}"? This will reset the current race state.
DBM_ERR_COPY_INACTIVE: You can currently only copy the active database. Please switch to it first.
DBM_ERR_DELETE_ACTIVE: Cannot delete the currently active database. Please switch to another database first.
DBM_ERR_EXISTS: A database with this name already exists
DBM_ERR_RESET_INACTIVE: You can only reset the active database. Please switch to it first.
DBM_HINT_SELECT: Select a database to view details.
DBM_HINT_SWITCH: Switch to this database to enable operations.
DE_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this driver?
DE_CONFIRM_DISCARD_MESSAGE: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?
DE_ERROR_NAME_EXISTS: Driver name or nickname already exists
DE_HELP_AVATAR_CONTENT: Click here to select a profile picture for this driver.
DE_HELP_DUPLICATE_CONTENT: Quickly duplicates the current driver profile with an increment layout.
DE_HELP_NAME_CONTENT: Enter the full display name for the driver.
DE_HELP_NICKNAME_CONTENT: Add a unique nickname used for identification in scoring layouts.
DE_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: On this page you'll configure your drivers. Edits will automatically be saved as long as the driver name and nickname are unique.
DE_TOOLTIP_NAME_EXISTS: Driver name or nickname already exists
DM_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this driver?
DM_HELP_CREATE_CONTENT: Select this button to create a new driver.
DM_HELP_DELETE_CONTENT: Select this button to delete this driver.
DM_HELP_DETAIL_CONTENT: This is the summary of the selected driver. It shows the driver name, image, and audio configuration.
DM_HELP_EDIT_CONTENT: Select this button to edit the selected driver.
DM_HELP_HELP_CONTENT: Select this button to show this guided help at any time.
DM_HELP_SIDEBAR_CONTENT: This shows a list of all your available drivers. Selecting one will show a summary of the driver and allow you to edit it.
DM_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: This provides top level information on all your available drivers and allows you to edit and create new ones.
RDS_ANALYTICS_LOCAL_DISABLED: Client and Server analytics is disabled
RDS_ANALYTICS_LOCAL_ENABLED: Client and Server analytics is enabled
RDS_ANALYTICS_SYNC_ERROR: Failed to synchronize server analytics setting
RDS_ERR_DRIVER_DUPE_IND_TEAM: The driver '{{driver}}' is participating as an individual and also as a member of '{{team}}'.
RDS_ERR_DRIVER_DUPE_TEAMS: The driver '{{driver}}' is participating on multiple teams: {{teams}}.
RDS_ERR_START_RACE_FIX_DESCRIPTION: Please remove the redundant driver or team before starting.
RDS_HELP_ANALYTICS_CONTENT: When enabled, RaceCoordinator AI will try to report usage data that will ever be used to make the software better. This data tracking requires an internet connection and will not interfere with the perfomance of the software. You can toggle it on/off using this icon on virtually any screen within the software.
RDS_HELP_DRIVER_ACTIONS_CONTENT: Use these buttons to add all drivers to the race, remove all drivers from the race, or ranomize the driver order (seeding).
RDS_HELP_DRIVER_SELECTION_CONTENT:
Here you can select who will be racing.

Available Drivers: All drivers in your database.
Selected Drivers: Drivers participating in this race. Drag and drop to reorder or set starting positions.
RDS_HELP_RACE_SELECTION_CONTENT: Select the race format you want to run.
RDS_HELP_RECENT_RACE_CONTENT: Click to select a recently run race.
RDS_HELP_RECENT_RACE_MOST_RECENT_CONTENT: Click to select the most recently run race.
RDS_HELP_START_DEMO_CONTENT: When you are ready, use this button to test your configuration with out a track interface.
RDS_HELP_START_RACE_CONTENT: When you are ready, begins the official race with the selected configuration.  Your track interface must be configured and turned on.
RDS_HELP_WALKTHROUGH_CONTENT: You can click here on any page to start the walkthrough over again.
RDS_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: This is where you configure and start your races. You can select drivers, choose a race format, and customize settings.
RDS_PROMPT_LOAD_AUTOSAVE: An auto-save exists for this race. Do you want to load it?
RDS_QUOTE_1: “The development of full artificial intelligence could spell the end of the human race….It would take off on its own, and re-design itself at an ever increasing rate. Humans, who are limited by slow biological evolution, couldn’t compete, and would be superseded.” — Stephen Hawking
RDS_QUOTE_10: “Some people call this artificial intelligence, but the reality is this technology will enhance us. So instead of artificial intelligence, I think we’ll augment our intelligence.” — Ginni Rometty
RDS_QUOTE_11: “I’m more frightened than interested by artificial intelligence – in fact, perhaps fright and interest are not far away from one another. Things can become real in your mind, you can be tricked, and you believe things you wouldn’t ordinarily. A world run by automatons doesn’t seem completely unrealistic anymore. It’s a bit chilling.” — Gemma Whelan
RDS_QUOTE_12: “You have to talk about ‘The Terminator’ if you’re talking about artificial intelligence. I actually think that that’s way off. I don’t think that an artificially intelligent system that has superhuman intelligence will be violent. I do think that it will disrupt our culture.” — Gray Scott
RDS_QUOTE_13: “If the government regulates against use of drones or stem cells or artificial intelligence, all that means is that the work and the research leave the borders of that country and go someplace else.” — Peter Diamandis
RDS_QUOTE_14: “The key to artificial intelligence has always been the representation.” — Jeff Hawkins
RDS_QUOTE_15: “It’s going to be interesting to see how society deals with artificial intelligence, but it will definitely be cool.” — Colin Angle
RDS_QUOTE_16: “Anything that could give rise to smarter-than-human intelligence—in the form of Artificial Intelligence, brain-computer interfaces, or neuroscience-based human intelligence enhancement – wins hands down beyond contest as doing the most to change the world. Nothing else is even in the same league.” — Eliezer Yudkowsky
RDS_QUOTE_17: “Artificial intelligence is growing up fast, as are robots whose facial expressions can elicit empathy and make your mirror neurons quiver.” — Diane Ackerman
RDS_QUOTE_18: “Someone on TV has only to say, ‘Alexa,’ and she lights up. She’s always ready for action, the perfect woman, never says, ‘Not tonight, dear.’” — Sybil Sage
RDS_QUOTE_19: “Some people worry that artificial intelligence will make us feel inferior, but then, anybody in his right mind should have an inferiority complex every time he looks at a flower.” — Alan Kay
RDS_QUOTE_2: “I visualise a time when we will be to robots what dogs are to humans, and I’m rooting for the machines.” — Claude Shannon
RDS_QUOTE_20: “Artificial intelligence will reach human levels by around 2029. Follow that out further to, say, 2045, we will have multiplied the intelligence, the human biological machine intelligence of our civilization a billion-fold.” — Ray Kurzweil
RDS_QUOTE_21: “Nobody phrases it this way, but I think that artificial intelligence is almost a humanities discipline. It’s really an attempt to understand human intelligence and human cognition.” — Sebastian Thrun
RDS_QUOTE_22: “A year spent in artificial intelligence is enough to make one believe in God.” — Alan Perlis
RDS_QUOTE_23: “There is no reason and no way that a human mind can keep up with an artificial intelligence machine by 2035.” — Gray Scott
RDS_QUOTE_24: “Is artificial intelligence less than our intelligence?” — Spike Jonze
RDS_QUOTE_25: “By far, the greatest danger of Artificial Intelligence is that people conclude too early that they understand it.” — Eliezer Yudkowsky
RDS_QUOTE_26: “The sad thing about artificial intelligence is that it lacks artifice and therefore intelligence.” — Jean Baudrillard
RDS_QUOTE_27: “Forget artificial intelligence – in the brave new world of big data, it’s artificial idiocy we should be looking out for.” — Tom Chatfield
RDS_QUOTE_28: “Before we work on artificial intelligence why don’t we do something about natural stupidity?” — Steve Polyak
RDS_QUOTE_29: AI is one small step for race management systems, and one large step towards my retirement. --Dave
RDS_QUOTE_3: “Artificial intelligence would be the ultimate version of Google. The ultimate search engine that would understand everything on the web. It would understand exactly what you wanted, and it would give you the right thing. We’re nowhere near doing that now. However, we can get incrementally closer to that, and that is basically what we work on.” — Larry Page
RDS_QUOTE_4: “The pace of progress in artificial intelligence (I’m not referring to narrow AI) is incredibly fast. Unless you have direct exposure to groups like Deepmind, you have no idea how fast—it is growing at a pace close to exponential. The risk of something seriously dangerous happening is in the five-year time frame. 10 years at most.” — Elon Musk
RDS_QUOTE_5: “The upheavals [of artificial intelligence] can escalate quickly and become scarier and even cataclysmic. Imagine how a medical robot, originally programmed to rid cancer, could conclude that the best way to obliterate cancer is to exterminate humans who are genetically prone to the disease.” — Nick Bilton
RDS_QUOTE_6: “I don’t want to really scare you, but it was alarming how many people I talked to who are highly placed people in AI who have retreats that are sort of ‘bug out’ houses, to which they could flee if it all hits the fan.” — James Barrat
RDS_QUOTE_7: “I’m increasingly inclined to think that there should be some regulatory oversight, maybe at the national and international level, just to make sure that we don’t do something very foolish. I mean with artificial intelligence we’re summoning the demon.” — Elon Musk
RDS_QUOTE_8: “The real question is, when will we draft an artificial intelligence bill of rights? What will that consist of? And who will get to decide that?” — Gray Scott
RDS_QUOTE_9: “We must address, individually and collectively, moral and ethical issues raised by cutting-edge research in artificial intelligence and biotechnology, which will enable significant life extension, designer babies, and memory extraction.” — Klaus Schwab
RDS_START_RACE_TOOLTIP: Add at least one driver before starting the race.
RD_ERROR_NO_DRIVERS: No drivers selected for the race.
RD_ERR_DRIVER_CHANGE_NOT_IN_PIT: Driver change rejected: Car is not in the pit
RD_TAGLINE: The Future of Slot Car Racing
RE_AUTO_ADVANCE_WARMUP_TIME_HINT: The track power will turn on for the last X seconds of the auto-advance timer.
RE_AUTO_START_WARMUP_TIME_HINT: The track power and lap recording will stay on for the first X seconds of the auto-start timer for a warmup session.
RE_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this race?
RE_CONFIRM_DISCARD_MESSAGE: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?
RE_DRIVER_COUNT_HINT: Changes the number of drivers used in the example heat list. This does not affect the race configuration.
RE_END_HEAT_ON_OUT_OF_FUEL: End Heat on Out of Fuel
RE_MIN_LAP_TIME_HINT: Lap times below this value will be accumulated (0 to ignore).
RM_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this race?
RM_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: Here you can manage your races. Select a race from the list to view its summary setup.
TEM_CONFIRM_DISCARD_MESSAGE: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?
TEM_HELP_ASSIGNED_CONTENT: Drag and drop to reorder the team driver lineup.
TEM_HELP_AVAILABLE_CONTENT: Click to assign a driver to this team.
TEM_HELP_AVATAR_CONTENT: Click to select or upload a team logo image.
TEM_HELP_DUPLICATE_CONTENT: Create a copy of this team with a unique name.
TEM_HELP_NAME_CONTENT: Enter a unique name for your team.
TEM_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: Welcome to the Team Editor. Manage team details and assign drivers.
TE_CONFIRM_DISCARD_MESSAGE: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?
TE_HELP_ADD_INTERFACE_CONTENT: Select this button to add a new interface to the track using factory defaults. The interface must then be configured in the panel to the right.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_ANALOG_SELECTOR_CONTENT: Similar to the digital pins, these are the analog pin assignments. Some behaviors such as voltage dividers can only be assigned to analog pins.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_ANALOG_STATUS_CONTENT: Works exactly the same as the digital pins status icon.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_BOARD_TYPE_CONTENT: Select which board you have. This has no impact on the connection, but will limit the configuraiton interface to only things the particular board type supports.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_COM_PORT_CONTENT: Confgure which serial (COM) port to conntect to. This field must be set to the correct serial port for your interface or no data will flow between the RMS and the interface
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_CONTENT: Use this section to configure your arduino based track interface.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_DEBOUNCE_CONTENT: Input pin debounce time in micro seconds. Any time the input pin goes from high to low to high or low to high to low within this time, the state change is ignored as noise. It's not common to need to increase this but anything under 1000 should be unnoticable during a race
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_NC_RELAYS_CONTENT: Selects the type of relay used on your track. NC relays are recommended as you'll have track power even if the RMS is not running. If track power is off when it should be on and on when it should be off, often switching this value will solve the problem.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_NC_SENSORS_CONTENT: When set, the sensor is read as a high signal when the car is not triggering the sensor. Photo sensors and IR sensors are two examples of NC sensors. Dead strips and read switches likely need this setting turned off. If during a fuel race, cars are continuously refueling, changing this setting will usually fix the issue.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_PIN_SELECTOR_CONTENT: Assign digital pins to various track behaviors, including lap counting, relays, callbuttons, segment timing sensors, etc. Set to unused of nothing is actually wired to the pin or undefined behaviors will be seen. Set to reserved if you've implemented custom behaviors in the arduino sketch that use this pin
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_PIN_STATUS_CONTENT: This icon indicates the state of the pin that is assigned to a behavior. If the pin is an input like a lap sensor, triggering the lap sensor will cause the icon to go from grey to green for a moment. If the pin is an output such as a relay, clicking on the icon will toggle the state of the pin. This behavior only works if the status icon for the serial connection is green.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_PIT_BEHAVIOR_CONTENT: You can define an extra behavior for any pin assigned to detect laps. Usually this is set to pit in/out so that the lap sensors can be used for refueling during a fuel race, but you can also assign it to other pit behaviiors for more advanced setups
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_STATUS_CONTENT: This icon indicates the status of this interface. If grey, there is no connection. Check to see that the arduino is loaded with the correct sketch, plugged into your PC, powered on, and that the COM port is configured correctly. If green, data is flowing between the RMS and the arduino interface. Nothing will work on the interface until this status badge is solid green
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_VOLTAGE_CONTENT: Voltage dividers can be used to detect the throttle level for individual lanes. This allows features like digital fuel in the race configuration to work, however you must configure the dividers so that the interface knows how to interpret the level it reads in.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_VOLTAGE_LINK_CONTENT: Click this icon to link or unlink all lanes together. When linked, a change in one lane will be reflected in all lanes. When unlinked each lane configuration works independently.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_VOLTAGE_LIVE_CONTENT: This value shows the current value read by the divider. Setting your controller to full throttle will tell you what the max voltage should be set to. This only works if the serial connection status is green and the divider is properly wired and configured.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_VOLTAGE_MAX_CONTENT: Set the value read in from the divider that the interface should interpret as full throttle. This value must be between [1, 1023].
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_VOLTAGE_RESET_CONTENT: Select this button to reset the maximum value seen on all lanes.
TE_HELP_ARDUINO_VOLTAGE_SET_MAX_CONTENT: This value shows the maximum voltage seen for the lane thus far. This is useful if the level is fluctuating you don't have to see the highest level to know what it is. Clicking on this button sets the max voltage to the value shown when it was selected.
TE_HELP_DELETE_LANE_CONTENT: Select this button to delete this lane from the track.
TE_HELP_DUPLICATE_CONTENT: Make an exact copy of this track, except for the name which must be unique
TE_HELP_GENERAL_CONTENT: Changes made on this page are automatically saved unless the configuration is invalid, in which case saving is halted until corrections are made.
TE_HELP_INTERFACES_CONTENT: This shows a list of available interfaces you can use to connect to your track. Click the button to the right of the interface you'd like to use.
TE_HELP_LANES_CONTENT: This section shows all lane currently configured. Using the selectors for each lane you can add/remove/edit the lane configuration
TE_HELP_LANE_BG_CONTENT: Select this button to change the lanes background color
TE_HELP_LANE_DELETE_CONTENT: Select this button to remove the lane from the track setup.
TE_HELP_LANE_DRAG_CONTENT: Grab here to change the lane ordering by dragging and dropping it into its new position.
TE_HELP_LANE_FG_CONTENT: Select this button to change the lanes foreground color
TE_HELP_LANE_LENGTH_CONTENT: Edit this value to set the tracks lane length. Enter a value in feet. This value will be used for miles per hour and kilometers per hour speed calculations. Note: 1 meter = 3.28084 feet, and 1 foot = 0.3048 meters.
TE_HELP_NAME_CONTENT: You can change the name using this box. The name must be unique to all other tracks you may have created or your configuration is invalid.
TE_HELP_REDO_CONTENT: Click here to redo a change you previously undid. This is also mapped to the Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Z keyboard shortcut.
TE_HELP_UNDO_CONTENT: Click here to undo your last change. This is also mapped to the Cmd/Ctrl+Z keyboard shortcut.
TE_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: On this page you'll configure the lane on your track and the interface you have connected to your track. You must setup the lanes and interface before you can run a race with your track hardware.
TE_NO_INTERFACES_DESC: Add an interface from the General section on the left.
TMM_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this team?
TMM_HELP_CREATE_CONTENT: Select this button to create a new team.
TMM_HELP_DELETE_CONTENT: Select this button to delete this team.
TMM_HELP_DETAIL_CONTENT: This is the summary of the selected team. It shows the team name, image, and a list of drivers on the team.
TMM_HELP_EDIT_CONTENT: Select this button to edit the selected team.
TMM_HELP_HELP_CONTENT: Select this button to show this guided help at any time.
TMM_HELP_SIDEBAR_CONTENT: This shows a list of all your available teams. Selecting one will show a summary of the team and allow you to edit it.
TMM_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: This provides top level information on all your available teams and allows you to edit and create new ones.
TM_CONFIRM_DELETE: Are you sure you want to delete this track?
TM_HELP_CREATE_CONTENT: Select this button to create a new track using factory defaults.
TM_HELP_DELETE_CONTENT: Select this button to delete this track.
TM_HELP_DETAIL_CONTENT: This is the summary of the selected track. It shows which track is selected, a summary of the interfaces attached to the track, and lane information for the track.
TM_HELP_EDIT_CONTENT: Select this button to edit the selected track.
TM_HELP_HELP_CONTENT: Select this button to show this guided help at any time.
TM_HELP_SIDEBAR_CONTENT: This shows a list of all your available tracks. Selecting one will show a summary of the track and allow you to edit it.
TM_HELP_WELCOME_CONTENT: This provides top level information on all your available tracks allows you to edit them.
UE_CONFIRM_DISCARD_MESSAGE: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?
UE_REORDER_HINT: Drag values to column anchors to customize the layout. CenterCenter is the primary value.
